title: cymbopogon hirtus subsp. villosus
intro: Cymbopogon hirtus subsp. villosus, also known as wild lemongrass or coarse lemongrass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is traditionally used in medicine and culinary practices due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Cymbopogon hirtus subsp. villosus have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
